conjecture: Eisenstein integer norm is multiplicative N(alpha*beta) = N(alpha)*N(beta) for all integer pairs
decomposition_raw: [{"id": "S1", "statement": "For all pairs of Eisenstein integers $\alpha, \beta$, $N(\alpha\beta) = N(\alpha) \cdot N(\beta)$", "verifier": "norm_multiplicative", "args": {"N": 1000}, "reason": "This verifier directly tests the multiplicative norm identity across randomly sampled Eisenstein integer pairs, which validates the conjecture as the identity is a polynomial equation in the integer coordinates defining Eisenstein integers, making random sampling a sound local verification method."}]
